title: Jenkins agents disconnect intermittently during long builds
symptoms: Build log ends with 'FATAL: channel is already closed'
Agent status flips to offline then reconnects a minute later
root_cause: JNLP keepalive interval longer than the idle timeout of the load balancer
Agent pod hits memory limit and the kernel OOMs the JVM
Controller under-provisioned causing event loop backpressure
diagnosis: Inspect controller logs for 'Ping failed. Terminating the channel'
kubectl top pod on the agent pod during the build
Check LB idle timeout (AWS NLB default is 350s)
fix: Raise LB idle timeout to 3600s and set `-XX:MaxRAMPercentage=75`
Scale controller CPU and separate build logs onto an EBS volume
prevention: Track build duration SLO and alert on channel-closed errors
Use ephemeral Kubernetes agents with requests/limits aligned
Enable JCasC so controller config is reproducible

title: GitHub Actions workflow exposes secrets in logs
symptoms: Secret value visible in the job log output
GitHub secret scanner raises an alert
PR from a fork triggered the workflow
root_cause: Workflow uses pull_request_target and checks out the untrusted PR code
Secrets injected through `run: echo $SECRET` without masking
Third-party action was pinned to a moving tag that was compromised
diagnosis: Check action SHAs against the original repo
fix: Rotate the leaked secret immediately in the settings UI and provider
Switch to `pull_request` trigger and require `permissions: read-all`
prevention: Enable required reviewers on environments containing production secrets
Use OIDC federation with AWS/GCP instead of long-lived tokens
Run gitleaks in CI to block accidental commits
